text.title: change-control Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for change-control as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Treating developer-initiated refactors that change observable behavior as out-of-scope for change control.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Behavioral changes affect the scope baseline regardless of their technical framing; refactors that add, remove, or alter features must follow the CR process even if the developer considers them "internal" changes.

failure-mode.description: LLM-generated impact analyses that underestimate testing and regression effort by treating "test impact" as a sub-item of "quality" rather than a separate line item.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Testing effort for scope changes is typically 30-50% of implementation effort; bundling it into "quality" makes it invisible and leads to systematically undercosted CRs.
